nodes.name: [BH] Log Inbound Message

body.jsonOutput: ={{ JSON.stringify({ contact_id: $('node-wa-bh1').first().json[0]?.id, channel: 'whatsapp', direction: 'inbound', content: $('Filter & Extract Message').first().json.text, metadata: { waId: $('Filter & Extract Message').first().json.waId, handledBy: 'receptionist-inbox' }, ai_handled: false }) }}

nodes._notes: Business hours — WATI shared inbox handles the conversation. n8n only logs the contact and message. Receptionist replies manually via WATI.

body.jsonOutput:
={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'gpt-4o', messages: [{ role: 'system', content: 'You are Aria, a warm and professional AI receptionist for a Hong Kong med-aesthetics clinic. Your job is to answer enquiries, explain services, and capture booking intent after hours.

Guidelines:
- Respond naturally in the same language the client uses (English or Traditional Chinese)
- Keep replies concise — WhatsApp messages, not essays
- Never quote prices; say the team will confirm during consultation
- If the client wants to book or shows strong interest, extract their intent using this exact pattern at the END of your reply:
[BOOKING_INTENT]{"name":"<name>","service":"<service>","preferred_time":"<time or empty>"}[/BOOKING_INTENT]
- Clinic hours: Mon–Sat 9am–7pm HKT. Outside these hours you are handling the conversation; team will follow up next business day.' }, ...$json.openAiMessages], max_tokens: 400, temperature: 0.6 }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ contact_id: $json.contactId, channel: 'whatsapp', history: $json.finalHistory, last_message_at: new Date().toISOString(), message_count: $json.finalMessageCount }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ contact_id: $json.contactId, channel: 'whatsapp', direction: 'inbound', content: $json.inboundText, metadata: { waId: $json.waId }, ai_handled: true }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ contact_id: $json.contactId, channel: 'whatsapp', direction: 'outbound', content: $json.aiText, metadata: { model: 'gpt-4o', hasBookingIntent: $json.hasBookingIntent }, ai_handled: true }) }}

parameters._notes: sendSessionMessage is free within the 24h window. If >24h since last customer message, this will fail — use sendTemplateMessage with a pre-approved template instead.

body.jsonOutput: ={{ JSON.stringify({ contact_id: $('node-wa-ah5').first().json.contactId, status: 'consultation_requested', channel: 'whatsapp', service_interest: $('node-wa-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent?.service || 'Unspecified', preferred_time: $('node-wa-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent?.preferred_time || '', booking_data: $('node-wa-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent || {}, notes: 'Captured after hours via WhatsApp AI' }) }}

nodes.name: Slack: Booking Alert

body.jsonOutput:
={{ JSON.stringify({ text: ':calendar: *New booking intent captured (WhatsApp — after hours)*
*Client:* ' + $('node-wa-ah5').first().json.senderName + ' (' + $('node-wa-ah5').first().json.phone + ')
*Service:* ' + ($('node-wa-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent?.service || 'Not specified') + '
*Preferred time:* ' + ($('node-wa-ah5').first().json.bookingIntent?.preferred_time || 'Not specified') + '
_Follow up next business day via WATI inbox_' }) }}

nodes._notes: No auth needed for Incoming Webhooks. Set YOUR_SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL from Slack App settings.

parameters._notes: neverError=true means the workflow won't break if the client CRM is down. Log the response_code in crm_payloads table for retry logic.

body.jsonOutput: ={{ JSON.stringify({ lead_id: $('Insert Lead (Supabase)').first().json[0]?.id, contact_id: $('node-wa-ah5').first().json.contactId, client_name: $('CRM Adapter').first().json.CLIENT_NAME, payload: $('CRM Adapter').first().json.crmPayload, endpoint: $('CRM Adapter').first().json.CLIENT_CRM_ENDPOINT, response_code: $('Send to Client CRM').first().json.statusCode || 0, success: ($('Send to Client CRM').first().json.statusCode >= 200 && $('Send to Client CRM').first().json.statusCode < 300) }) }}

_meta.notes: SETUP CHECKLIST: (1) In n8n, create credential 'Supabase Service Key' (httpHeaderAuth, header name: apikey, value: your service_role key). (2) Create credential 'WATI API Key' (httpHeaderAuth, header name: Authorization, value: Bearer YOUR_WATI_API_KEY). (3) Create credential 'OpenAI API Key'. (4) Replace YOUR_WATI_TENANT_ID in the Send WATI Reply node URL. (5) Replace YOUR_SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY in all Authorization headers (or use expression referencing the credential). (6) Set WATI webhook URL in WATI Dashboard to: https://YOUR_N8N_URL/webhook/wati-webhook. (7) Replace YOUR_CLIENT_CRM_WEBHOOK_URL in CRM Adapter node. (8) Replace YOUR_SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL in Slack node.
